summary:
Only cefoperazone effectively targets Pseudomonas aeruginosa based on current data; others like ceftizoxime or cefixime show less promising results for treating infections caused by this bacterium.
```
To summarize concisely while focusing solely on critical aspects related directly to addressing pseudomonal resistance within a two-sentence framework as requested:

Cephalexin, cefuroxime, and aztreonam are ineffective against \emph{P.\ aeruginosa}, but cefoperazone demonstrates significant antibacterial action specifically targeting this organism due to its unique pharmacodynamics versus other cephalosporins evaluated here. ```plaintext Summary: Among common cephalosporins, only cefoperazone has proven effective against Pseudomonas aeruginosa according to available studies; other agents such as cefalexin and cefuroxime fail to provide adequate coverage against this resistant strain of bacteria. ```
This summary captures essential details about which specific drug works well compared to alternatives without extraneous detail.
```
